name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with feet planted. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width.
Unrack and hold the bar over your mid-chest with locked elbows.
Lower the bar to your sternum with control, tucking elbows around 45 degrees.
Press the bar back up in a slight arc until elbows lock.

conditionNotes.note: Press symmetrically. If asymmetric loading worsens curvature, prefer dumbbells or a machine.
Reduce range of motion if shoulder or wrist pain flares. A slight incline and neutral wrist often help.
Horizontal pressing is beneficial for upper-body bone density. Keep load controlled.
Plant feet firmly. Arching is fine but avoid extremes. Keep lower back in contact with the bench.
Avoid heavy maximal loading. Exhale during the press and avoid prolonged breath-holding.
Resistance training improves glucose uptake. Keep intensity moderate and monitor energy levels.
Use a spotter or Smith machine for safety. Controlled tempo helps manage tremors.
Avoid locking elbows at the top. Keep active tension throughout the movement.
Avoid supine pressing after the first trimester. Use incline or cable alternatives.
Start with reduced load and shorter ROM. Use dumbbells or a machine for safer loading.
